title: Minimum Operations to Make Array Equal II
description:
You are given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2 of equal length n and an integer k. You can perform the following operation on nums1:

*   Choose two indexes i and j and increment nums1[i] by k and decrement nums1[j] by k. In other words, nums1[i] = nums1[i] + k and nums1[j] = nums1[j] - k.

nums1 is said to be equal to nums2 if for all indices i such that 0 <= i < n, nums1[i] == nums2[i].

Return _the minimum number of operations required to make_ nums1 _equal to_ nums2. If it is impossible to make them equal, return -1.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def min_changes(nums1, nums2, k):
    n = len(nums1)
    operations = 0
    for i in range(n):
        diff = abs(nums1[i] - nums2[i])
        if diff % k != 0:
            return -1
        operations += diff // k
    return operations
```
    
    The algorithm iterates through both input arrays and computes the absolute difference between corresponding elements in `nums1` and `nums2`. If any difference is not divisible by `k`, it is impossible to make both arrays equal, so the function returns `-1`. Otherwise, the function adds the required number of operations (`diff / k`) to the total operations counter. After iterating through all the elements, the function returns the total number of required operations.
